name: No-Fail Bagels
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the yeast in a glass with 1/2 cup warm water. Stir in the sugar until dissolved.
2. Let stand for 5 minutes, until foamy on top.
3. Pour this mixture into a bowl and add 2 cups lukewarm water. Stir in the salt.
4. Add the bread flour one cup at a time, mixing with a wooden spoon after each addition. The dough will be sticky.
5. Spread 1/4 cup of remaining flour on a work surface. Place the dough on the floured surface and sprinkle 1/4 cup flour on top.
6. Knead slowly until the flour combines with the rest of the dough, then knead vigorously for 15 minutes. Add more flour if needed to avoid stickiness.
7. Cut the dough into 16 equal portions using a sharp knife dipped in flour. Roll each portion into a ball and make a hole about the size of a quarter by poking your thumb through the center.
8. Spread cornmeal on two trays or cutting boards. Place 8 formed bagels on each tray, about 1 inch apart. Cover with a clean dish towel and place in a warm, draft-free spot for 45 minutes to rise.
9. Remove the towel and place the trays of bagels in the refrigerator for 1 hour.
10. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C). Boil water in a large pot.
11. Sprinkle yellow cornmeal on two cookie sheets.
12. Place 4 bagels at a time into the boiling water. The perfect bagel should sink and rise immediately. Boil for about 4 minutes, turning every 30 seconds.
13. Remove the bagels with a slotted spoon and let drain excess water on a clean towel for a few seconds.
14. Place 8 bagels on each cookie sheet.
15. Add your favorite topping (sesame, garlic, or onion) if desired.
16. Bake for 35 minutes or until golden brown. Remove from the cookie sheets and cool on a wire rack.
